frames.title: Transportation Profitability Estimation
frames.subtitle: A calculator-first operating view for owner-operators.
bullets: Built for user-supplied freight assumptions.
Designed to keep cost, miles, and margin visible.

frames.title: Enter The Load See The Pressure
frames.subtitle: Revenue, miles, fuel, FSC, overhead, and reserve assumptions in one workflow.
bullets: Manual inputs remain the calculator authority.
Route and timing context stay attached to the load.

frames.title: True RPM After Deadhead
frames.subtitle: Loaded RPM alone can hide unpaid movement. True RPM keeps total trip miles visible.
bullets: All values are estimates from entered assumptions.
Profitability is interpreted, not guaranteed.

frames.title: Deadhead Changes The Whole Load
frames.subtitle: Route intelligence frames movement pressure, stop complexity, and distance imbalance.
bullets: Deadhead is unpaid operating distance.
Pickup-to-delivery timing shapes earning capacity.

frames.title: Fuel Cost Meets FSC Recovery
frames.subtitle: Fuel surcharge improves recovery, but it does not erase inefficient miles or bad assumptions.
bullets: MPG, speed, weight, idle time, and terrain affect real-world outcomes.
Use actual receipts later to compare estimate vs. trip reality.

frames.title: Estimate First Actuals After
frames.subtitle: Capture diesel, DEF, tolls, parking, scale tickets, repairs, hotel, and miscellaneous trip costs.
bullets: Actual result stays separate from estimated analysis.
Trip expenses explain margin movement after the load is done.

frames.title: Save The Snapshot Review The Pattern
frames.subtitle: Saved loads preserve entered assumptions, calculated outputs, and later actuals for review.
bullets: History supports operational awareness over time.
Saved records do not change the original calculator authority.

frames.title: Know Your Cost Before The Load
frames.subtitle: Vehicle, overhead, reserve, and target RPM settings help keep the calculator grounded.
bullets: Profile values are assumptions the operator controls.
Better inputs make estimates more useful.

frames.title: Understand What The Fields Mean
frames.subtitle: Contextual guidance explains inputs, outputs, and operational meaning without changing formulas.
bullets: Atlas support is educational and informational.
The deterministic LoadIQ calculator remains the source of truth.

frames.title: Freight Economics Route Context
frames.subtitle: Atlas Freight and Route views interpret calculated outputs after the load result exists.
bullets: Atlas does not dispatch, route, or guarantee outcomes.
It helps explain what the entered numbers suggest.

frames.title: Built For The Road
frames.subtitle: Fast estimate review, compact result cards, and saved-load access from the operating rhythm.
bullets: Designed for practical field use.
Still an estimation platform, not operational control software.

frames.title: Built On The Road One Mile At A Time
frames.subtitle: Karpilo LoadIQ with Karpilo Atlas AI educational and analytical support.
bullets: Transportation profitability estimation.
Operational awareness without replacing operator judgment.
